demographic_audit_note: Audited Atlantis demographics against Northcrest face sheet; corrected patient's zip code and added daughter's secondary mobile number. Verified Medicare Advantage PPO eligibility.
home_vs_ltc_determination: SW confirms the goal is Home with HHA support; patient has strong family involvement but requires a hospital bed and O2.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2 Check-in: SW reports patient is stable post-chemo rehab. PT goals met for transfers. Discharge target set for Friday. DME vendor confirmed for Thursday delivery.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card inserted into patient's phone; program flyer delivered to daughter (POA) during bedside education session on Day 1 expectations.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Pulse call conducted 24h prior to D/C. Daughter reports 'DME Failure': the hospital bed arrived but is missing the mattress and side rails. Home is currently unsafe for transition.
atlantis_final_sync: D/C date adjusted to 11/18; 1st visit date 11/19 entered into Atlantis following DME resolution.
ma_visit_booking: MA scheduled for 11/19 at 09:00 AM to ensure visit occurs within 24 hours of the rescheduled discharge.
narrative_summary: Patient was triaged in Atlantis and confirmed for home discharge with HHA services. During the maintenance phase, education was provided to the family and contact info was exchanged. A significant DME delivery failure was identified during the 24-hour pulse call, requiring a 3-day discharge delay which the PN communicated to the home team. Once the correct equipment was verified at the home, the MA visit was successfully scheduled for the morning after discharge.

format_1_state_log.event_description: DME_Delivery_Failure: Hospital bed arrived incomplete (no mattress/rails).
PN verifies correct DME setup via photo confirmation from daughter.

format_2_triples.situation: Daughter reports incomplete hospital bed delivery during pulse call.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Immediately flagged the DME mismatch to the Social Worker and HHA intake coordinator.

format_2_triples.situation: Patient's daughter expresses anxiety about managing the O2 concentrator.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Walked family through Day 1 expectations, including who to call if the HHA nurse is delayed.

format_2_triples.situation: Discharge date shifted due to DME delay.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Updated Atlantis D/C and First Visit dates to align with the new 24-hour window.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Wait for the Social Worker to provide a new confirmed discharge date after they resolve the DME issue with the vendor.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW boundary as the primary coordinator of facility-ordered equipment and discharge timing.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Coordinate with the HHA intake coordinator to put the start-of-care visit on 'standby' and verify they have the capacity to shift the visit to the following Monday if the DME is not fixed by Friday.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Aligned with Stage 3 checklist for HHA/Team Handoff and de-conflicting the schedule to protect the 24-hour visit window.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Call the DME vendor directly to provide clinical justification for an emergency mattress delivery and suggest a different vendor to the SW if they cannot deliver today.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Oversteps into SW territory (finding agencies/vendors) and clinical medication/equipment management.
